summary: The pytest smoke test run crashes with a fatal segmentation fault while executing tests in numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py. All tests in test__exceptions.py passed successfully before the process was killed by SIGSEGV. The build-time meson feature-probe errors are expected noise and did not prevent the build from completing.
root_cause: A segmentation fault in the Python interpreter (or a C extension it called) terminates the test process mid-run during execution of numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py.

evidence.reason: This is the direct cause of the CI failure â€” the Python process crashed with a SIGSEGV, aborting the test run.
The segfault occurs inside test_datetime.py after several test cases pass (13 dots visible), pinpointing the file in which the crash is triggered.
All tests in the preceding file passed cleanly, confirming the build and import machinery are functional and the crash is isolated to test_datetime.py.

suggested_fix: Reproduce locally by running `pytest numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py -x` under a debug build or with a memory-error sanitizer (e.g. `PYTHONFAULTHANDLER=1`) to identify which specific test and C-extension call triggers the segfault, then inspect the relevant C code for an out-of-bounds access or use-after-free.
